title: Carrot and walnut cake
subtitle: Spiced carrot cake with cream cheese frosting
excerpt: A moist, spiced carrot cake made with oil rather than butter, packed with grated carrot and walnuts, and finished with a thick cream cheese frosting. Serves ten.
sourceNotes: Carrot cake in its modern form derives from wartime British and American recipes that used carrots to add sweetness to baking in the absence of sufficient sugar. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: a carrot pudding made with grated carrot, flour, suet, and spice is documented as a traditional British preparation. USDA Cooperative Extension Service publications on vegetable baking document carrot cake as a widely reproduced American domestic preparation using oil, eggs, sugar, and grated carrots, noting that the oil (rather than butter) is key to the moist crumb.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted, cooled cake wrapped in cling film for up to 2 months. Frost after thawing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the next day once the spices have had time to develop.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf; the cake is done when a skewer through the centre comes out clean and the top is deep gold

content.text: Oil rather than butter keeps the crumb moist for days; butter sets hard when cold, oil doesn't. Use a neutral oil such as sunflower or vegetable. The carrot should be coarsely grated — fine-grated disappears into the batter and loses its texture.

Preheat the oven to 170°C fan. Grease and line a 23 cm round tin. Whisk the {{plain-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{mixed-spice}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} together in a large bowl. Whisk the {{light-brown-sugar}} and {{eggs}} in a separate bowl until combined, then pour in 200 ml neutral oil and whisk until smooth. Fold the wet ingredients into the dry until nearly combined, then fold in the {{carrot}} and {{walnuts}} until just mixed. Pour into the prepared tin and bake for 42–47 minutes until a skewer through the centre comes out clean.

Cool the cake in the tin for 20 minutes, then turn out onto a rack and cool completely. Beat the {{cream-cheese}} and frosting {{unsalted-butter}} together until smooth. Add the sifted {{icing-sugar}} and {{vanilla-extract}} and beat until just combined; stop before it turns runny (cream cheese frosting breaks if overbeaten). Spread generously over the cooled cake. Refrigerate for 30 minutes before serving to set the frosting.
